intro: Fagelia fothergillii, also known as Fothergilla, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its white, fragrant flowers and attractive foliage. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in soothing respiratory issues and supporting immune function. Bioactive constituents of Fothergilla include alkaloids, phenolic compounds, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Fothergilla is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in topical creams and salves to support skin health and respiratory wellness.
